title: Publications
text:
PLOS ONE · May 2024


Automatic quantification of tumor-stroma ratio as a prognostic biomarker for pancreatic cancer


Vendittelli P, et al. Multi-class U-Net for TSR segmentation across 368 patients / 4 datasets (25 NL labs). Dice 0.75 (epithelium) and 0.86 (tumor bulk) on external TCGA-PAAD validation. TSR AUC 0.61 for 6-month survival prediction on an independent cohort.




MIDL 2023 · Sep 2023 · Poster


Automatic quantification of TSR as a prognostic marker for pancreatic cancer


Vendittelli P, et al. Segmentation of tumor epithelium, healthy epithelium, and TSR estimation from pancreatic whole-slide images.




Cancers (MDPI) · Jul 2022 · Review


Setting the research agenda for clinical AI in pancreatic adenocarcinoma imaging


Vendittelli P, et al. Systematic review of the current role of AI for pancreatic cancer diagnosis and personalised treatment planning.




SPIE Medical Imaging · Apr 2022 · Poster


Automatic tumour segmentation in H&E-stained whole-slide images of the pancreas


Vendittelli P, et al. Multi-task U-Net with auxiliary classification head for coarse pancreatic tumour area segmentation; classification head reduced false positives.




European Radiology · 2025


End-to-end prognostication in pancreatic cancer by multimodal deep learning: a retrospective, multicenter study


Schuurmans M, Saha A, Alves N, Vendittelli P, et al. Multimodal deep learning combining CT imaging and clinical data for PDAC outcome prediction across multiple centers.




The Lancet Oncology · 2025


Artificial intelligence and radiologists in pancreatic cancer detection using standard of care CT scans (PANORAMA): an international, paired, non-inferiority, confirmatory, observational study


Alves N, Schuurmans M, Rutkowski D, Saha A, Vendittelli P, et al. International paired non-inferiority study comparing AI and radiologists for pancreatic cancer detection on standard-of-care CT.




Medical Image Analysis · 2025 · Review


Navigating the landscape of multimodal AI in medicine: a scoping review on technical challenges and clinical applications


Schouten D, Nicoletti G, Dille B, Chia C, Vendittelli P, et al. Scoping review on technical challenges and clinical applications of multimodal AI in medicine.




COMPAYL Workshop, MICCAI 2025 · Sep 2025 · Poster


Combining AI-based Biomarkers for PDAC Survival


Hensens L, Sabroso-Lasa S, Verbeke C, Malats N, Litjens G, Vendittelli P. Multi-tissue segmentation pipeline integrating TSR, mitosis density, stromal cell density and tumor-to-tissue ratio for multivariate survival prediction in pancreatic cancer.

title: Projects
text:
Personal Project · 2024 – Present


Weekly AI Paper-Triage Agent


Multi-agent pipeline (OpenAI API, Python): weekly arXiv / PubMed scan → LLM summarisation → interest-based scoring (0–10) → automated email digest. In active personal use since 2024. Demonstrates production LLM-agent design: scheduler, structured output, email delivery, and prompt-engineered relevance scoring.
GitHub




Open Source Framework


Pathology Segmentation Pipeline


A Docker-based pipeline for training and deploying deep learning segmentation models on Whole Slide Images (WSI). Built around an asynchronous tile inference engine, it supports tissue/background segmentation, multi-tissue classification, epithelium segmentation, and tumour-stroma ratio (TSR) computation for Pancreatic Cancer WSIs. The pipeline is also available as a pre-built image on DockerHub at pierpaolov93/pathology-pipeline:latest.
Tech: PyTorch · Docker · HuggingFace · Python
GitHub DockerHub




PhD Research · Radboud UMC · 2021 – 2024


Pancreatic Cancer WSI Segmentation & Prognosis (PANCAIM)


End-to-end pipeline for whole-slide image analysis of pancreatic cancer within the EU Horizon 2020 PANCAIM consortium. Multi-class U-Net achieving Dice 0.75 / 0.86 on external validation across 368 patients from 25 Dutch labs. TSR-derived biomarker yielded AUC 0.61 for 6-month survival prediction.
Tech: PyTorch · MONAI · Docker · SLURM · Python
GitHub




Postdoc Research · Prinses Maxima Centre · 2024 – Present


Wilms Tumor Anaplasia Grading


Deep-learning and multiple-instance learning pipelines for anaplasia detection and clinical outcome prediction from pediatric kidney tumor WSIs. Multi-center dataset integration across European collaborating hospitals.
Tech: PyTorch · MIL · Docker · multi-center data pipelines
GitHub




Master’s Thesis · University of Girona · 2020


MRI Synthesis: ResU-Net vs. GAN Benchmarking


Benchmarked ResU-Net, cGAN, and CycleGAN for T1↔︎FLAIR MRI synthesis on WMH and BraTS 2018 datasets. ResU-Net achieved superior SSIM and lower MSE under data-scarce conditions. Grade: 8.6/10.
Tech: Keras · TensorFlow · Python · medical image registration

title: Curriculum Vitae
text:
Experience


Dec 2024 – Present


Postdoc Researcher — Prinses Maxima Centre for Pediatric Oncology, Utrecht, NL

Developing deep-learning and MIL pipelines for WSI analysis of Wilms tumor, targeting anaplasia grading and clinical outcome prediction.
Coordinating multi-center dataset integration across collaborating hospitals.
Translating model outputs into interpretable biomarkers in collaboration with pathologists and pediatric oncologists.
Supervising students; contributing to peer-reviewed publications.





Mar 2021 – Jun 2026 (exp.)


PhD Candidate — AI for Digital Pathology — Radboud University Medical Center, Nijmegen, NL

Developed deep-learning pipelines for pancreatic cancer WSI analysis within PANCAIM (EU Horizon 2020): multi-class U-Net for TSR quantification (368 patients / 4 datasets / 25 NL labs; Dice 0.75–0.86; TSR AUC 0.61 on TCGA-PAAD). Published in PLOS ONE (2024) and SPIE MI (2022).
Extended to multi-tissue segmentation for combining AI-based biomarkers (TSR, mitosis density, stromal cell density, TTR) in multivariate PDAC survival analysis. Published at COMPAYL (MICCAI 2025 workshop).
Co-authored multi-center clinical studies: European Radiology (2025) on multimodal prognostication; The Lancet Oncology (2025) PANORAMA study; Medical Image Analysis (2025) scoping review on multimodal AI.
Teaching Assistant for Image Analysis and Medical Imaging Analysis (4 course editions, Radboud University); co-supervised Master’s theses.





Oct 2020 – Feb 2021


Machine Learning Engineer — Everis Italy (NTT DATA), Rome, IT

Built AI chatbots and conversational systems for clients in banking and automotive.
Annotated industrial data for object recognition systems and developed live translation pipelines for video content.





Feb 2020 – Sep 2020


Research Intern — ViCOROB – NeuroImage Computing Group, University of Girona, ES

Benchmarked MRI synthesis architectures (ResU-Net, cGAN, CycleGAN) on WMH and BraTS 2018 datasets.
ResU-Net showed superior SSIM and lower MSE vs. GAN variants under data-scarce conditions; informed Master’s thesis (grade 8.6/10).

title: Pierpaolo Vendittelli
text:
Postdoc Researcher · Medical AI & Computational Pathology


📍 Utrecht, The Netherlands  ·  Open to Italy · Remote EU · US-remote

I build deep-learning systems for digital pathology — from whole-slide image segmentation to multi-center clinical AI pipelines. My current work focuses on Wilms tumor grading at Prinses Maxima Centre; my PhD at Radboud UMC produced validated models for pancreatic cancer prognosis (PLOS ONE 2024). I also design LLM-based automation tools for research workflows.
I am actively seeking R&D / Applied Scientist / ML Engineer roles (finishing PhD June 2026).

View CV Publications Projects GitHub

title: Pierpaolo Vendittelli
text:
Research Interests



Computational Pathology
Whole-slide image analysis, multi-instance learning, segmentation, and survival prediction in oncology.




Medical AI
End-to-end pipelines for clinical deployment — reproducible, multi-center, interpretable.




LLM Agents & Automation
Production LLM pipelines for research workflows (paper triage, summarisation, scoring).
